name: 🔍🛠️ Tavily Search & Extract - Template
description: Automated workflow: 🔍🛠️ Tavily Search & Extract - Template. This workflow integrates 7 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, chainLlm, set, stopAndError. It contains 26 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

originalWorkflowJson.name: 🔍🛠️ Tavily Search & Extract - Template

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Tavily API Search Endpoint

**Base URL**: `{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}`
**Method**: POST

### Required Parameters
- `query`: The search query string
- `api_key`: Your Tavily API key

### Optional Parameters
- `search_depth`: "basic" or "advanced" (default: "basic")
- `topic`: "general" or "news" (default: "general") 
- `max_results`: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5)
- `include_images`: Include query-related images (default: false)
- `include_answer`: Include AI-generated answer (default: false)
- `include_raw_content`: Include parsed HTML content (default: false)
- `include_domains`: List of domains to include
- `exclude_domains`: List of domains to exclude
- `time_range`: Filter by time range ("day", "week", "month", "year")
- `days`: Number of days back for news results (default: 3)

### Example Request
```json
{
    "api_key": "tvly-YOUR_API_KEY",
    "query": "Who is Leo Messi?",
    "search_depth": "basic",
    "include_answer": false,
    "include_images": true,
    "max_results": 5
}
```

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Tavily API Extract Endpoint 

**Base URL**: `{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}`
**Method**: POST

### Required Parameters
- `urls`: Single URL string or array of URLs
- `api_key`: Your Tavily API key

### Optional Parameters
- `include_images`: Include extracted images (default: false)

### Example Request
```json
{
    "api_key": "tvly-YOUR_API_KEY", 
    "urls": [
        "{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}",
        "{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}"
    ]
}
```

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## Tavily API Documentation

The Tavily REST API provides seamless access to Tavily Search, a powerful search engine for LLM agents, and Tavily Extract, an advanced web scraping solution optimized for LLMs.

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

The Tavily API provides two main endpoints for search and data extraction.

The API returns JSON responses containing:

- Search results with titles, URLs, and content
- Extracted raw content from specified URLs
- Response time metrics
- Any error messages for failed requests


**Note**: Error handling should check for failed results in the response before processing.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Tavily API Overview
{{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

The Tavily API provides a specialized search engine built specifically for AI agents and LLM applications, offering two main endpoints:

## Search Endpoint

The search endpoint enables intelligent web searching with:

**Key Features**
- Query-based search with customizable depth ("basic" or "advanced")
- Topic filtering for general or news content
- Control over result quantity and content type
- Domain inclusion/exclusion capabilities
- Time range filtering and news date restrictions

## Extract Endpoint

The extract endpoint focuses on content retrieval:

**Key Features**
- Single or batch URL processing
- Raw content extraction
- Optional image extraction
- Structured response format

## Implementation Benefits

**For AI Integration**
- Optimized for RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation)
- Single API call handles searching, scraping and filtering
- Customizable response formats
- Built-in content relevance scoring

**Technical Advantages**
- JSON response format
- Error handling for failed requests
- Response time metrics
- Flexible content filtering options


This API is designed to simplify the integration of real-time web data into AI applications while ensuring high-quality, relevant results through intelligent processing and filtering.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details

Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Tavily Extract" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Tavily Extract Top Search" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Summarize Web Page Content" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get Top Result".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Filter > 90%".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Tavily API Key".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
